nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation.

nodes.title: Seminar in Neuroscience
nodes.description: Research presentations and critical review of the literature in various areas of anatomical and neurobiological sciences.
nodes.other_requirements: NGP Graduate Students only or by instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Human Gross and Microanatomy
nodes.description: Combination of gross anatomy, histology, embryology, physiology and medical imaging to present an integrated overview of the human body. Emphasis on peripheral nervous system including autonomic nervous system and cranial nerves. Cadaver dissection laboratory combined with lecture and/or content modules and research and teaching presentations. Pre/Co-requisites: Six credits coursework, plus two credits lab in biology, general chemistry, organic chemistry and physics; Neuroscience Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Six credits coursework, plus two credits lab in biology, general chemistry, organic chemistry and physics; Neuroscience Graduate student or Instructor permission. Graduate standing; Neuroscience Graduate Program or others with instructor permission.

nodes.title: First-Year NSCI Seminar
nodes.description: Introduces first-year neuroscience majors to the field of neuroscience by introducing students to different perspectives and subfields through researchers and students at UVM.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Co-requisite: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 2
nodes.description: Part II of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body. Prerequisite: ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: Common hour exams are held 5:30-7:00pm on these Mondays: 2/9, 3/2, and 4/6. BIOM,CSD,DIET,DNFS,EXSC,HSCI,MLS,NFS, PE, PHS, MRS, PRNU; Majors only until Dec. 2nd, Please contact Patrick Mullen (patrick.c.mullen@uvm.edu) with any questions; Class meets 3x a week with online lab comprising 4th credit; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for ANPS 1200 ZRA; Prereq enforced by the system:ANPS 1190.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Intro to Psychological Science
nodes.description: Introduction to the entire field, emphasizing the behavior of the normal adult human being.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Cellular Neurophysiology
nodes.description: Fundamentals of cellular neurophysiology through lecture, independent student reading and faculty-led group discussions of journal articles. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 3220 and NSCI 5220. Prerequisites: NSCI 2100 or NSCI 2105 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience w/lab
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. With lab. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Pre/Co-requisites: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. Must register for a lab: NSCI 2100 A01-A03. For lecture only course, see NSCI 2105. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Resp Conduct in Biomed Rsch
nodes.description: Topics in Scientific Integrity surrounding responsible conduct and practices in biomedical research. Prerequisites: Advanced Graduate students, postdoctoral fellows and assistant professors in the biological or biomedical sciences.
nodes.other_requirements: Advanced Graduate students, postdoctoral fellows and assistant professors in the biological or biomedical sciences. Grad students only; Grad students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for NSCI 6270 ZRA

nodes.title: Diseases of the Nervous System
nodes.description: Senior level, seminar-style capstone course in which students bring together information learned in other courses for an in-depth study of disease states of the nervous system. Prerequisites: NSCI 2100 or NSCI 2105; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience Lab
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 1
nodes.description: Part I of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereg limited to: BMCS, BIOM, CSD, EMS, EXSC, HSCI, MLS, MRS, NFS, NMT, PE, PHS, PRNU majors only. Degree students only even after major restrictions lifted.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include matter, stoichiometry, gas laws, thermochemistry, quantum theory, atomic structure, electronic configurations, bonding, and intermolecular forces. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: Medical Neuroscience Part 1
nodes.description: Explores the nervous system through integrative study of behavior, cellular and systems neurobiology, neuroanatomy, neuroethics, neuropharmacology, neurophysiology, pathophysiology, and psychopathology. Several instructional methods support learning in this course, including lecture, online independent study modules, laboratory sessions, team-based learning and case and problem based discussions. Prerequisites: Neuroscience Graduate student; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience Graduate student; Instructor permission. Term B; 8 weeks; Neuroscience Graduate Students only; Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Human Neuroanatomy
nodes.description: Functional anatomy of the human nervous system on both the microscopic and macroscopic scales. Focuses on the structures of the peripheral nervous system, spinal cord, and brain, and how they work together to achieve behavior. Lectures and a required laboratory (gross and microscopic anatomy). Prerequisite: NSCI 2105, NSCI 2100, or ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Invertebrate Neurobiology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Neuroscience
nodes.description: Functional anatomy of the human nervous system. Lectures and laboratory providing learning experience with dissected specimens, gross and microscopic anatomy. Incorporates clinical information from physician-scientists. Prerequisite: Physical Therapy Graduate student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Physical Therapy Graduate student or Instructor permission. OT & PT majors only. Others with instructor permission.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 2
nodes.description: Principles of organismal biology; nature of scientific inquiry, plant form and function, pollination ecology, animal phylogeny illustrated by comparative anatomy and physiology; animal behavior. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1450 and BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: ASCI, BIOL, BISC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL majors should register for BCOR 1450; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre med students register for BIOL 1450

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Graduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate students only; Instructor permission
